title: Sheinbaum Defies U.S. Pressure, Upholds Cuban Medical Worker Agreement
detail: President Claudia Sheinbaum announced at her morning press conference (mañanera) that Mexico will continue to accept Cuban doctors and medical specialists in the country, defying mounting U.S. pressure that has caused other Latin American nations to abandon similar agreements. 'It's a bilateral agreement that helps Mexico a lot,' Sheinbaum said, adding that Cuban doctors fill critical gaps in rural areas where Mexican specialists are unwilling to work. The Trump administration had characterized the Cuban medical program as akin to forced labor and has been pressuring hemisphere-wide governments to sever the agreements.

media.caption: Sheinbaum defies US pressure, upholds Cuban medical worker agreement

title: Mexico Continues Backing Bachelet for UN Secretary-General Post
detail: President Sheinbaum reaffirmed Mexico's support for former Chilean president Michelle Bachelet's candidacy for UN Secretary-General, even as Chile withdrew its own support the previous day. 'We believe that Bachelet is the ideal person to lead the United Nations, and we will continue to support her,' Sheinbaum told her daily press conference. Bachelet served as Chile's first female head of state, UN High Commissioner for Human Rights from 2018–2022, and Executive Director of UN Women from 2010–2013. She is among the main candidates to succeed Secretary-General António Guterres.
